revision.text:
[[ja:大願寺 (廿日市市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Daikanji 2011 1.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Hatsukaichi|qid=Q736897|1=Hatsukaichi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.2955|N|132.318167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|1=Benzaiten}}|part_of={{ill|Q11363344|qid=Q11363344|1=Chūgoku 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage}}; {{ill|Q11484682|qid=Q11484682|1=88 Sacred Sites of Shikoku}}; {{ill|Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten|qid=Q17214976|1=Three Great Shrines of Benzaiten}}}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 大願寺 |image = [[File:Daikanji 2011 1.JPG|250px]]
山門 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[廿日市市]][[厳島|宮島町]]3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|17|43.8|N|132|19|5.4|E|scale:10000_region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} |山号 = 亀居山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[薬師如来]]
[[弁才天]] |創建年 = |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = [[中国四十九薬師霊場]]22番
[[広島新四国八十八ヶ所霊場]]1番 |文化財 = 木造[[薬師如来]]像（重要文化財）
木造[[釈迦如来]]坐像（重要文化財）
[[阿難]]尊者像（重要文化財）
[[迦葉]]尊者像（重要文化財）
紙本墨書尊海渡海日記（八曲屏風裏書、表に紙本墨画山水図（瀟湘八景図）あり） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} '''Daiganji''' (大願寺) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Shingon Koyasan Sect|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} temple located in {{ill|Itsukushima|en|Itsukushima|qid=Q875301|lt=Miyajima, Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|1=Itsukushima}}, {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|qid=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|1=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. The temple's mountain name is Kamegasen (亀居山), and its cloister name is Hōkō-in (放光院). The principal deity is {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. The main hall houses a hidden statue of {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}, which was transferred from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}} policy. This hidden statue is unveiled only once a year on June 17th. == History == The exact founding date of the temple is unknown. However, temple tradition states that it was founded in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}. Another theory suggests that it was founded in the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} during the Kennin era ({{ill|1201|en|1201|qid=Q5225|lt=1201|1=1201}}–{{ill|1204|en|1204|qid=Q5228|lt=1204|1=1204}}) by {{ill|Ryōkai|ja|了海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōkai|1=Ryōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30318209}}. The temple has historically maintained a close relationship with {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishō-in (Miyajima)|1=Daishō-in (Miyajima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438538}} and Itsukushima Shrine. == Temple Grounds == The main hall contains the hidden eight-armed Benzaiten statue transferred from Itsukushima Shrine, with {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amitābha|1=Amitābha}} and the former principal deity of the Gomadō, {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|en|Cintāmaṇicakra|qid=Q11446712|lt=Nyoirin Kannon|1=Cintāmaṇicakra}}, enshrined as flanking attendants. Among the oldest surviving Buddhist statues on Miyajima is a wooden statue of {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Other significant statues include: * The former principal statue of the Senjō-kaku hall, a wooden seated statue of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} (Important Cultural Property), with accompanying figures of {{ill|Ānanda|en|Ānanda|qid=Q28988|lt=Ānanda|1=Ānanda}} and {{ill|Mahākāśyapa|en|Mahākāśyapa|qid=Q335304|lt=Kassapa Buddha|1=Mahākāśyapa}} (both Important Cultural Properties). * The principal statue of the Gojū-no-tō (Five-story Pagoda), a seated Shakyamuni Tathāgata Buddha flanked by {{ill|Manjushri|en|Manjushri|qid=Q471696|lt=Manjushri|1=Manjushri}} and {{ill|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|en|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|qid=Q868306|lt=Samantabhadra|1=Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)}}. * The principal statue of the Tahōtō (Two-story Pagoda), a seated Yakushi Nyorai. The inner hall also includes a study where {{ill|Katsu Kaishū|en|Katsu Kaishū|qid=Q700470|lt=Katsu Kaishū|1=Katsu Kaishū}} and samurai of the {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q876267}} held discussions during the Second Chōshū Expedition. Within the temple grounds is a shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}, the divine messenger of Yakushi Nyorai. In 2006 ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 18), the temple reconstructed the Gomadō hall, enshrining a 1.6-meter-high statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} carved from white sandalwood. == Cultural Properties == * Important Cultural Properties: ** Wooden seated statue of {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} ** Wooden seated statue of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} ** Wooden standing statue of {{ill|Ānanda|en|Ānanda|qid=Q28988|lt=Ānanda|1=Ānanda}} ** Wooden standing statue of {{ill|Mahākāśyapa|en|Mahākāśyapa|qid=Q335304|lt=Kassapa Buddha|1=Mahākāśyapa}} ** Calligraphy of "Senkai Tōkai Diary" on a folding screen (featuring an ink painting of the "Eight Views of Xiaoxiang") == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Chūgoku 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|ja|中国四十九薬師霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūgoku 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|1=Chūgoku 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363344}} :21 {{ill|Fudō-in (Hiroshima)|ja|不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō-in (Hiroshima)|1=Fudō-in (Hiroshima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361657}} → '''22 Daiganji''' → 23 {{ill|Keigetsu-in|ja|渓月院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keigetsu-in|1=Keigetsu-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q38278433}} == Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Daishō-in (Miyajima)|ja|大聖院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishō-in (Miyajima)|1=Daishō-in (Miyajima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438538}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Miyajima Public Aquarium|en|Miyajima Public Aquarium|qid=Q6764196|lt=Miyajima Aquarium|1=Miyajima Public Aquarium}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|qid=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen|1=Mount Misen}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|qq=}} * [https://itsukushima-daiganji.com/ Official Website] link|Q11441893}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Chūgoku 49 Yakushi Pilgrimage Sites]] Cultural Property of Japan in Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]]
